mechanism: Raindrop impact + air bubble entrainment + crown splash ensemble
ecosystem: Open water (Puget Sound, Salish Sea, coastal bays)
status: Third entry in meteorological arc 249-252; softest meteorological entry

drop_impact_stages: air bubble entrainment (1mm bubble → ~14,000 Hz oscillation; musical ring element)
surface wave (low-frequency ripple from each impact)
acoustic_physics.bubble_frequency: f ≈ 14,000 Hz for 1mm bubble; ~20,000 Hz for 0.5mm bubble
acoustic_physics.ensemble_effect: Thousands of simultaneous impacts produce sustained white noise; not pure white noise -- textured by drop size distribution and rainfall rate variation

rainfall_rate_spectrum.moderate_1_to_5mm_hr: Overlapping impacts, continuous sound with textural variation -- PNW signature
rainfall_rate_spectrum.heavy_over_10mm_hr: Dense enough to produce near-pure white noise

acoustic_physics.e3_placement: Moderate, continuous PNW rain is present but not dominant. Softer than wind in forest (E=4-5). The most constant is the quietest.

drop_size.contrast: Smaller than continental convective storm drops (up to 5-6 mm). Gentler, more persistent quality.

puget_sound_context.saltwater_effect: Slightly higher surface tension, different bubble dynamics vs. fresh water
puget_sound_context.ferry_system: ~24 million passengers/year. Many experience rain-on-water regularly from ferry decks and terminals.

acoustic_ecology.keynote_sound: Auditory background of PNW winter; brain habituates to it as constant. Most clearly heard when it stops.
acoustic_ecology.neurological_resonance: Overlaps with pink/brown noise that promotes sleep. Commercial white noise machines approximate this profile.
acoustic_ecology.cultural_loading: Most immediately place-evocative sound in the engine; universal PNW place-memory
acoustic_ecology.seasonal_context: Seattle wettest: Nov-Jan. Summer relatively dry. Rain is fundamentally a winter phenomenon.

theory_nodes.concept: THE SIGNATURE PNW SOUND. Pacific rain on Puget Sound water surface: sustained white noise with texture generated by drop impact + air bubble entrainment (1mm bubble → ~14,000 Hz). E=3 -- softest meteorological entry, but most culturally loaded. 150-160 Seattle rain days/year. The acoustic signature of the Pacific Northwest experience; keynote sound of the PNW winter; the background all other sounds are heard against.
theory_nodes.description: Cultural loading and acoustic physics of PNW rain.

theory_nodes.concept: Meteorological arc energy variation: 249 (E=4) → 250 (E=5) → 251 (E=3) → 252 (E=4). Rain is the softest. The most constant is the quietest. E=3 is accurate for the moderate persistent rain that characterizes PNW climate; more dramatic entries would be inaccurate to the actual daily experience.
theory_nodes.description: E=3 as accurate placement in arc context.

cross_references.relationship: Wind (249, 250) → Rain (251). Meteorological arc progression from wind to precipitation. Rain at E=3 is softer than wind at E=4-5.
Rain (E=3) → Tidal surge (E=4). Meteorological to hydrological. Water in atmosphere (rain) to water on land (tidal surge). Energy rises in final entry.

s36_pair.pairing_note: E=3 vs E=7. Most ambient vs. most kinetic. Sustained diffuse white noise vs. focused garage rock attack. Polar contrast achieving E=10 equilibrium.
